messages.content: You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can log in with a valid email and password.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Login with valid credentials", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter a registered email", "Enter the correct password", "Click Login"], "expected": "User is logged in and redirected to the dashboard"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Login with wrong password", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter a registered email", "Enter an incorrect password", "Click Login"], "expected": "Error 'Invalid email or password' is shown; user stays on login page"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Login with empty fields", "type": "negative", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Leave email and password blank", "Click Login"], "expected": "Validation messages prompt for required fields"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Email is case-insensitive", "type": "edge", "priority": "low", "steps": ["Enter the email in UPPERCASE", "Enter correct password", "Click Login"], "expected": "Login succeeds; email matching ignores case"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can reset password via an email link that expires in 15 minutes.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Request reset for valid email", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Click 'Forgot password'", "Enter registered email", "Submit"], "expected": "Reset email is sent; confirmation message shown"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Reset link works within 15 minutes", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Open reset link within 15 min", "Set a new password", "Submit"], "expected": "Password is updated; user can log in with new password"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Expired reset link is rejected", "type": "boundary", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Open reset link after 15 min", "Attempt to set new password"], "expected": "Link is rejected with 'This link has expired' message"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Reset for unregistered email", "type": "negative", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Enter an email that is not registered", "Submit"], "expected": "Generic message shown (no account disclosure) for security"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can upload a profile picture (JPG/PNG only, max 5 MB).

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Upload valid 2 MB JPG", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Choose a 2 MB .jpg file", "Click Upload"], "expected": "Image uploads and displays as the profile picture"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Reject unsupported file type", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Choose a .gif or .pdf file", "Click Upload"], "expected": "Error 'Only JPG/PNG allowed'; upload blocked"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Reject file over 5 MB", "type": "boundary", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Choose a 6 MB .png file", "Click Upload"], "expected": "Error 'File exceeds 5 MB limit'; upload blocked"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Upload exactly 5 MB file", "type": "boundary", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Choose a file of exactly 5 MB", "Click Upload"], "expected": "Upload succeeds (limit is inclusive)"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can apply a discount coupon at checkout; expired coupons are rejected.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Apply a valid coupon", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Add items to cart", "Enter a valid coupon code", "Apply"], "expected": "Discount is applied; total updates correctly"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Apply an expired coupon", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter an expired coupon code", "Apply"], "expected": "Error 'Coupon expired'; total unchanged"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Apply an invalid code", "type": "negative", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Enter a random invalid code", "Apply"], "expected": "Error 'Invalid coupon'; total unchanged"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Coupon valid only above minimum spend", "type": "edge", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Add items below the minimum spend", "Apply a min-spend coupon"], "expected": "Error explaining minimum spend not met"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can pay with a credit card; invalid card numbers are rejected.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Pay with a valid card", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter valid card number, expiry, CVV", "Submit payment"], "expected": "Payment succeeds; order confirmation shown"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Reject invalid card number", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter a card number failing Luhn check", "Submit"], "expected": "Error 'Invalid card number'; payment blocked"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Reject expired card", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter a card with a past expiry date", "Submit"], "expected": "Error 'Card expired'; payment blocked"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Reject wrong CVV length", "type": "boundary", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Enter a 2-digit CVV", "Submit"], "expected": "Validation error for CVV length"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: Admin can deactivate a user account, after which that user cannot log in.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Deactivate an active user", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Open user list", "Select an active user", "Click Deactivate"], "expected": "User status changes to 'Inactive'"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Deactivated user cannot log in", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Attempt login as the deactivated user"], "expected": "Login is blocked with 'Account deactivated' message"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Active session ends on deactivation", "type": "edge", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Deactivate a user who is currently logged in"], "expected": "User's active session is terminated on next request"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Reactivate restores access", "type": "positive", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Reactivate the user", "User attempts login"], "expected": "Login succeeds again"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: Reader can leave a comment (max 500 characters) on an article when logged in.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Post a valid comment", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Log in", "Type a 100-char comment", "Click Post"], "expected": "Comment appears under the article"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Reject comment over 500 chars", "type": "boundary", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Type a 501-char comment", "Click Post"], "expected": "Error 'Max 500 characters'; comment not posted"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Comment of exactly 500 chars", "type": "boundary", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Type a 500-char comment", "Click Post"], "expected": "Comment posts successfully (limit inclusive)"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Logged-out user cannot comment", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Log out", "Try to post a comment"], "expected": "User is prompted to log in; comment blocked"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can search products by keyword and filter by price range.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Search returns matching products", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter a keyword that matches products", "Submit search"], "expected": "Relevant products are listed"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Search with no matches", "type": "negative", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Enter a keyword with no matches", "Submit"], "expected": "'No results found' message shown"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Filter by price range", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Search a keyword", "Set min and max price", "Apply filter"], "expected": "Only products within the range are shown"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Min price greater than max price", "type": "edge", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Set min price higher than max price", "Apply"], "expected": "Validation message or auto-correction; no crash"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can enable two-factor authentication using an authenticator app.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Enable 2FA with valid code", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Open security settings", "Scan QR in authenticator app", "Enter the 6-digit code"], "expected": "2FA is enabled; backup codes shown"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Reject wrong 2FA code", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter an incorrect 6-digit code"], "expected": "Error 'Invalid code'; 2FA not enabled"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Login requires 2FA once enabled", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Log out", "Log in with password", "Enter 2FA code"], "expected": "Login completes only after correct 2FA code"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Expired TOTP code rejected", "type": "edge", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Enter a code from the previous time window"], "expected": "Code is rejected as expired"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can export transaction history for a date range as CSV.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Export a valid date range", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Select a start and end date with transactions", "Click Export CSV"], "expected": "CSV downloads with the correct rows and headers"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Export a range with no transactions", "type": "edge", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Select a range with no transactions", "Click Export"], "expected": "Empty CSV with headers only, or a clear 'no data' message"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "End date before start date", "type": "negative", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Set end date earlier than start date", "Click Export"], "expected": "Validation error; export blocked"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Special characters escaped in CSV", "type": "edge", "priority": "low", "steps": ["Export data containing commas/quotes in fields"], "expected": "Fields are properly quoted/escaped; CSV opens correctly"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: Shopper can add up to 10 items to the cart and see the total update.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Add an item updates total", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Add one item to the cart"], "expected": "Item count and total reflect the addition"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Add the 10th item", "type": "boundary", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Add items until the cart has 10"], "expected": "All 10 items are accepted; total correct"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Adding an 11th item is blocked", "type": "boundary", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Attempt to add an 11th item"], "expected": "Error 'Cart limit reached (10 items)'"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Remove item updates total", "type": "positive", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Remove an item from a non-empty cart"], "expected": "Total decreases accordingly"}]}
You are a senior QA engineer. Given a requirement, return JSON test cases covering positive, negative, edge, and boundary scenarios.
Generate test cases for: User can update their registered email address.

{"test_cases": [{"id": "TC-01", "title": "Update to a valid new email", "type": "positive", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Open account settings", "Enter a new valid email", "Save"], "expected": "Email is updated; verification sent to new address"}, {"id": "TC-02", "title": "Reject malformed email", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter 'user@@bad'", "Save"], "expected": "Validation error; email not changed"}, {"id": "TC-03", "title": "Reject email already in use", "type": "negative", "priority": "high", "steps": ["Enter an email belonging to another account", "Save"], "expected": "Error 'Email already in use'"}, {"id": "TC-04", "title": "Old email still works until verified", "type": "edge", "priority": "medium", "steps": ["Change email but do not verify", "Log in with old email"], "expected": "Old email remains valid until new one is verified"}]}
